I read the prompt and prior-turn log as an external proposal record. HenceDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the only direction compatible with the unit/radical argument: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Thus Da is radical, and the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. With this corrected Ps,h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)satisfies, for ő∂f=1, ő∂ÓÄ†=1,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and 2s+1‚Č°1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. The nonsingularity conclusion survives, but the quotient direction must be corrected.With these corrected formulas, the previously claimed finite-tame abelianization checks remain consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.2. Let Jr denote the indecomposable unipotent F2[C2a]-module of length r. For a‚Č•1, the nonfree part of L2His(L2H)nonfree‚ČÉJ1‚äē2‚äēJ2 a‚ąí1‚äēI(e,f),where

The two J1 summands are the universal square directions b[2]and S[2]. The J2a‚ąí1 -summands are indexed by involutions in őĒe,f, namelyŌĄňČjŌÉňČf/2,j(1+2f/2)‚Č°0(mode).

There are exactly gcd(e,2f/2+1) such j.This also explains why there are no other first-stage IA directions. The H-orbits in ‚ąß2F2 [őĒ] are described by unordered relative displacements d‚ąľd‚ąí1. A P-orbit of such pair-orbits is nonfree precisely when the displacement is an involution; every non-involutive displacement gives a free J2a -block and hence contributes no H1.

Since H1(C2a,Jr) has dimension 1 for r<2a and 0 for the free block J2a, this givesdimF2Ae,f=2+gcd(e,2f/2+1)for f even, and no residual even-Frobenius ambiguity after odd-Hall rigidity when f is odd.

3. Consequence for the live candidate The live candidate remains unrefuted. The corrected algebra preserves all finite-tame abelian, sign-kernel, and cup-product consistency checks recorded in the prior log. But the remaining proof gap is now sharper.A final proof must identify the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered P-operator action in the moduleJ1‚äē2‚äēJ2 a‚ąí1‚äēgcd(e,2f/2+1)and then in its higher restricted-power descendants. For (e,f)=(3,2), the three involution directions are the Zd classes already isolated in prior turns. For (e,f)=(5,4), there are five involution directions indexed by ŌĄjŌÉ2, plus the two universal square directions. This is the next genuinely discriminating arithmetic comparison.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected Turn 47‚Äôs trivial-block quotient: the valid elimination isPs(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,not the reciprocal. The sign-cover formula isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

With these corrected formulas, the conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4 remain consistent. I also refined the first marked IA ambiguity to the module statement(L2H)nonfree‚ČÉJ1‚äē2‚äēJ2 a‚ąí1‚äēgcd(e,2f/2+1).

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Compute the candidate‚Äôs actual coordinates in the two universal square directions and the involution-indexed directionsŌĄňČjŌÉňČf/2, starting with (e,f)=(5,4). Then compare those seven coordinates with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic for the corresponding tame degree-20 extension.[BREAKTHROUGH]
